name: Peter Matz
description: American musician, composer, arranger and conductor (1928–2002)
abstract: Peter Matz (November 6, 1928 – August 9, 2002) was an American musician, composer, arranger, and conductor. His musical career in film, theater, television, and studio recording spanned fifty years.

concerts.label: Rosemary Clooney and The Arrangers / A Tribute to Nelson Riddle

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section for Peter Matz and the ‘Kraft Music Hall” was Derek Smith, Mel Lewis and Richard Davis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: Peter Matz who I did an awful lot of stuff with Peter Matz... I got to know Peter really well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: I had known him when I was very young when he was going to UCLA I had done some work with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: there was Peter Matz, Irv Kostel, various of us who were musical directors for these shows... Peter Matz was the general conductor and orchestral arranger. I did the dance music for the various, whatever sequences needed to come up.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
